data.task:
Ticket 0013's daemon auto-reload mechanism is already IMPLEMENTED in packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts but has ZERO tests. Your atom is to write unit tests that PIN its behavior — especially the idle-only safety guarantee, which the ticket names as a required acceptance criterion. Do not reimplement the feature.

The mechanism (read it first, do not patch from a guess):
- isDaemonRuntimePath(file) (launcher.ts:173) matches packages/daemon/** and packages/core/**.
- scheduleDaemonReloadForRun(ctx, result) (launcher.ts:189) filters result.committedFiles by isDaemonRuntimePath; if any match, it merges them into ctx.daemonReload.pending, records a 'daemon-auto-reload-pending' event, then calls drainDaemonReload(ctx).
- drainDaemonReload(ctx) (launcher.ts:197) returns early while ctx.daemonReload.running OR ctx.inFlight.size > 0 OR no pending. Otherwise it runs ctx.buildDaemonForReload({cwd,timeoutMs}); on exitCode!==0 it records 'daemon-auto-reload-build-failed' + an audit line and returns WITHOUT restarting; on exitCode===0 it records '-build-succeeded', appends a 'daemon-auto-reload' audit, calls ctx.restartDaemon(), and records '-restart-queued'. The finally block re-drains if still idle and another reload is pending.
- Wiring: launchRun() chains scheduleDaemonReloadForRun onto runPromise.then(...) for BOTH ticket and priority runs (launcher.ts:506-516); attachRunLifecycle()'s .finally deletes ctx.inFlight then calls drainDaemonReload again (launcher.ts:352 then 371) — that is what opens the idle gate after the settling run leaves inFlight.

Write tests in packages/daemon/tests/ (follow the existing daemon test patterns — inject spies via createOzServer opts: restartDaemon, buildDaemonForReload, daemonReloadBuildTimeoutMs; assert on recorded events/audit). Prefer driving the real settle path through launchRun where practical; if that is too heavy, you may call the settle helpers directly, but at least the idle-only test must exercise the real ctx.inFlight gate, not a trivial stub. Cover these cases:
1. A run committing a packages/daemon/** file schedules and, once idle, runs buildDaemonForReload and (exitCode 0) calls restartDaemon; assert the pending/build-started/build-succeeded/restart-queued events.
2. A run committing a packages/core/** file ALSO triggers it (dependency coverage).
3. A run committing NO daemon/core files does NOT schedule: buildDaemonForReload and restartDaemon are never called.
4. IDLE-ONLY (load-bearing): a daemon-touching run that settles while another run is still in flight (ctx.inFlight.size > 0) does NOT reload — no build, no restart — and the reload fires only AFTER the last in-flight run settles. This is the 'no live-run interruption' guarantee.
5. BUILD-FAILURE: buildDaemonForReload returns a non-zero exitCode -> 'daemon-auto-reload-build-failed' event recorded AND restartDaemon is NEVER called (prior daemon survives).

Exit criterion (scripted): `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon test` is GREEN including your new tests, and the idle-only test genuinely asserts inFlight>0 deferral. Run it yourself and paste the result.

Write-scope: packages/daemon/tests/** only. Do NOT modify production code in packages/daemon/src/** unless a test surfaces a REAL bug — if it does, stop, fix the root cause minimally, and call it out explicitly in your summary. Must not break: existing daemon tests or unrelated behavior.

data.reason: pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon test is RED (1/244 failed): the load-bearing idle-only test times out at line 125 — blockFirstRunThenLetOthersFinishIO blocks whichever run calls awaitDirective first, which races with launch order, so the daemon-touching 'cocoder' run can itself get blocked and never settles. Make 'external' deterministically the in-flight/blocked run; suite must be fully green.

data.task:
Fix the ONE failing test in packages/daemon/tests/daemon-auto-reload.test.ts — 'daemon reload waits until all in-flight runs are idle'. The other 4 daemon-auto-reload tests pass, typecheck is clean, and the production code in packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts is correct — do NOT touch production code. The failure is a race in the test harness only.

Root cause: blockFirstRunThenLetOthersFinishIO() in packages/daemon/tests/helpers/daemon-reload-fixture.ts blocks whichever run calls awaitDirective FIRST. But awaitDirective call order is not deterministic relative to launch order, so the daemon-touching 'cocoder' run can become the blocked run instead of 'external' — then 'cocoder' never settles and the wait at daemon-auto-reload.test.ts:125 (!inFlight.has('cocoder') && inFlight.has('external')) times out.

Required behavior: 'external' must DETERMINISTICALLY be the in-flight/blocked run, and the daemon-touching 'cocoder' run must always run to completion (settling, scheduling the deferred reload) — regardless of awaitDirective call ordering. The safety assertions must stay exactly as-is in spirit: while 'external' is in flight, builds===0 and restarts===0 and the 'daemon-auto-reload-pending' event is recorded; after releasing 'external', the build runs exactly once with ctx.inFlight.size observed as 0 (buildObservedInFlight===[0]) and restartDaemon is called once.

Recommended mechanism (you may choose another that is equally race-free): make the helper expose an explicit blockRun(runId) setter and have EVERY awaitDirective call park on a 'designated' promise that resolves only once blockRun() has been called; then each call blocks iff its runId === the designated runId. The test then does: const externalRunId = await launch('external'); io.blockRun(externalRunId); const daemonRunId = await launch('cocoder'); ... This removes the ordering race entirely because both runs park until the test has designated which one is blocked, which happens before 'cocoder' is even launched. Do NOT block by 'first awaitDirective caller'.

Exit criterion (scripted, must be race-free not timing-luck): `pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon test` is GREEN (all 244 tests), and the idle-only test still genuinely asserts inFlight>0 deferral (build/restart deferred until external settles). Prove non-flakiness by running the suite 3 times in a row, all green, OR by making the mechanism fully deterministic (no reliance on awaitDirective ordering). Paste the results.

Write-scope: packages/daemon/tests/** only (the test file and its helper). Must not break: any other test.

data.reason: Race fixed via designatedBlockedRunIO parking every awaitDirective until blockRun() designates 'external'; pnpm --filter @cocoder/daemon typecheck clean and full suite 244/244 green 3x consecutively (non-flaky); idle-only test still asserts inFlight>0 deferral (buildObservedInFlight===[0]); tests-only, production code untouched.

data.task:
Build a one-command, founder-runnable proof harness at scripts/proof-daemon-reload.mjs that demonstrates ticket 0013's final acceptance bullet END-TO-END: after a run that adds a trivial new daemon route finalizes, the live daemon serves that new route with NO manual restart. The implementation already exists and is unit-proven (packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts: scheduleDaemonReloadForRun/drainDaemonReload, idle-gated; defaultBuildDaemonForReload + defaultRestartDaemon in server.ts). This atom adds the live proof the unit tests cannot give (they stub buildDaemonForReload/restartDaemon); USE THE REAL DEFAULTS here, do not stub the reload.

HARD SAFETY / ISOLATION CONSTRAINTS (non-negotiable — host/process safety):
- The harness must operate ONLY on an ISOLATED daemon: pick an ephemeral port (NOT 7878; e.g. COCODER_OZ_PORT in the 7900-7999 range or an OS-assigned free port) and an isolated temp cocoderHome (a copy/checkout of this repo, or a fixture wired to the real packages, under os.tmpdir()). It must NEVER start/stop/touch the founder's running daemon on :7878, never run `open`/OZ_OPEN, never drive cmux. The restart it triggers must target only its OWN ephemeral daemon (scripts/oz.sh free_port only kills the process on the port you set — set the ephemeral port in the child env).
- Add the trivial proof route ONLY inside the isolated temp working copy at runtime. Do NOT add a /proof route to the real committed packages/daemon/src — the real repo's daemon source must be unchanged by this run.
- Bounded watchdog: a wall-clock cap (e.g. 180s). On timeout or any failure, print FAIL + diagnostics (last ~20 lines of the temp daemon's oz.log) and exit non-zero. Missing-artifact rejection: if the reloaded daemon never answers /health or the new route 404s, FAIL clearly — never an infinite poll, never assume green from a check that did not run.
- ALWAYS clean up: kill the spawned ephemeral daemon and remove the temp dir in a finally block, on both success and failure.

FLOW the harness must execute:
1. Stand up the isolated temp cocoderHome and boot the real daemon bin there on the ephemeral port; capture its boot SHA; confirm /health.
2. Add a trivial new GET route to the daemon source in the temp home (e.g. GET /proof/daemon-reload -> 200 {\"ok\":true}) and git-commit it in the temp repo so HEAD advances past boot SHA (this is what a daemon-touching run commits).
3. Drive the reload through the REAL run-finalization path — i.e. exercise scheduleDaemonReloadForRun/drainDaemonReload with the real defaultBuildDaemonForReload (typecheck) and defaultRestartDaemon, with zero runs in flight (idle), so the daemon rebuilds-validates and re-execs onto the new source. Reuse the existing run/launch machinery where practical; if a full Bob/Oscar CLI run is impractical, programmatically produce a committed-files result touching packages/daemon/** and invoke the same settle->reload code the daemon uses (NOT a re-implementation, and NOT stubbed).
4. Poll (bounded) for the daemon to come back on the ephemeral port, then fetch http://127.0.0.1:<port>/proof/daemon-reload and assert HTTP 200 with the expected body — WITHOUT any manual restart in between.
5. Print a clear 'PROOF PASS' line and exit 0 on success; 'PROOF FAIL: <why>' + diagnostics and exit non-zero otherwise.

EXIT CRITERION (scripted): you run `node scripts/proof-daemon-reload.mjs` yourself and it prints PROOF PASS and exits 0, against its own ephemeral port, leaving the real repo source and the founder's :7878 daemon untouched. Paste the full output and the exit code. Also run `git status --short` afterward to prove no stray route was left in the real packages/daemon/src.

Write-scope: scripts/** (plus runtime temp dirs under os.tmpdir()). Do NOT modify packages/daemon/src/** production code. Must not break any existing test.

If the end-to-end process-restart proof proves genuinely infeasible to make robust within this atom (e.g. tsx/restart coordination from one script is too fragile), STOP and report exactly what blocks it and how far you got, rather than shipping a flaky or fake-green harness.

data.reason: node scripts/proof-daemon-reload.mjs prints PROOF PASS exit 0: isolated daemon on :7900 reloaded de5be02b->9ceda5d9 (boot SHA changed = real re-exec) and served /proof/daemon-reload via a real run using the real reload defaults, no manual restart; founder :7878 untouched, real packages/daemon|core src clean (only the two new scripts added).

data.task:
Documentation atom: two live governance docs reference ticket 0013 as OPEN/pending and cite the manual `scripts/oz.sh restart` bootstrap. 0013 has now LANDED this run (run_179): the Oz daemon auto-reloads after a run commits files under packages/daemon/** or packages/core/** — built ON TOP of the existing stale-daemon detection, idle-only (never interrupts an in-flight run; reload deferred until inFlight drains to zero), build-failure-surfaced (a failed typecheck leaves the prior daemon serving, never a dead daemon). It is unit-proven (packages/daemon/tests/daemon-auto-reload.test.ts, incl. the load-bearing idle-only test) and live-proven by a one-command harness (scripts/proof-daemon-reload.mjs: isolated ephemeral daemon reloads onto a newly-committed route, no manual restart). Make these edits ACCURATELY and MINIMALLY — do not overstate, do not change unrelated content:

1. docs/oz-hardening-owner-map.md (the paragraph at ~lines 214-217 about ticket 0013): update it so it no longer describes 0013 as open/pending 'adjacent infrastructure.' State that 0013 is DELIVERED (run_179) — the idle-only daemon auto-rebuild+reload landed on top of the stale-daemon detection, with unit + live proof (name scripts/proof-daemon-reload.mjs). Update the ticket path reference from cocoder/tickets/open/0013-... to cocoder/tickets/closed/0013-... since the ticket-close path moves it to closed/ on this run's successful completion. Keep the distinction-from-item-4 point if it still reads true, but as past-tense/delivered.

2. cocoder/PLAYBOOK.md (the `new-primary-root` entry, ~lines 236-240): the line 'Blocked on deploy: running daemon predates the fixes (ticket 0013 or manual scripts/oz.sh restart)' is now stale. Update ONLY the deploy-mechanism framing: the daemon now auto-reloads onto daemon-touching fixes once idle (ticket 0013 delivered, run_179), so the manual `scripts/oz.sh restart` bootstrap is no longer required for that gap. PRESERVE the remaining founder-verification semantics exactly — the live retest of job-hunt on a real external repo (billable, separate surface) still stands; do not mark new-primary-root done, do not change its CONTINUE status or other entries.

Acceptance: both edits are accurate to the shipped behavior (idle-only, build-failure-safe, unit+live proven), minimal, and touch nothing else. Write-scope: docs/oz-hardening-owner-map.md and cocoder/PLAYBOOK.md ONLY. Must not break: no code, no other docs, no other PLAYBOOK entries. Do NOT move or edit the ticket file itself (the ticket-close path handles that) and do NOT edit any test or source.

data.reason: Both doc edits accurate, minimal, correctly scoped (only docs/oz-hardening-owner-map.md + cocoder/PLAYBOOK.md changed): owner-map marks 0013 delivered run_179 with open->closed path and unit+live proof refs; PLAYBOOK new-primary-root updates only the deploy-mechanism line (manual restart no longer needed) and preserves CONTINUE status + founder-verification. No code/test touched; no behavior-pinned doc-link suite reads these files (verified); closed/0013 is the forward ref the ticket-close path fulfills on completion.

data.outcome: ✅ COMMITTED on `main` — 4 commit(s) on the active branch (no landing step; work is on the branch by construction). Committed out-of-lane (flagged, NOT held back): scripts/proof-daemon-reload-fixture.mjs, scripts/proof-daemon-reload.mjs, cocoder/PLAYBOOK.md, docs/oz-hardening-owner-map.md.
